claim: Create a PRD (Product Requirements Document) before writing code to establish scope and minimize assumptions; misalignment in planning causes exponentially more code errors than bad implementation
actionable: Draft a structured PRD with MVP scope, out-of-scope items, directory structure, and implementation phases before starting implementation with your coding agent
source_title: My COMPLETE Agentic Coding Workflow to Build Anything (No Fluff or Overengineering)

claim: Use sub-agents for research and exploration to isolate context pollution; main agent should focus on implementation while sub-agents load hundreds of thousands of tokens for research but only return summaries
actionable: Delegate research, codebase exploration, and web scraping to sub-agents in separate context windows; only return findings summaries to the main agent
source_title: My COMPLETE Agentic Coding Workflow to Build Anything (No Fluff or Overengineering)

claim: Context is your most precious resource when working with AI coding agents; implement strict context management by resetting conversations and using progressive disclosure with on-demand context files
actionable: Create reference folders with task-specific documentation that agents read only when needed; start new contexts for planning vs implementation phases
source_title: My COMPLETE Agentic Coding Workflow to Build Anything (No Fluff or Overengineering)

claim: Commandify everything: create reusable slash commands for repeated workflows (like /create-prd, /create-rules, /commit) so you don't repeat instructions and can easily evolve commands over time
actionable: For any multi-step process you do more than twice, create a slash command with structured prompts and templates
source_title: My COMPLETE Agentic Coding Workflow to Build Anything (No Fluff or Overengineering)

claim: Use the PIV loop (Plan, Implement, Validate) for each feature phase: create detailed task lists and validation strategies before implementation to force alignment, then delegate all coding
actionable: For each feature phase, create a structured plan with task list and detailed validation strategy (including end-to-end testing flows) before starting implementation
source_title: My COMPLETE Agentic Coding Workflow to Build Anything (No Fluff or Overengineering)

claim: Ask coding agents to ask YOU questions during planning (using ask user question tools) to reduce assumptions; agents are good at reasoning about edge cases and details humans might miss
actionable: In planning conversations, explicitly ask the agent to generate a flurry of clarifying questions, especially multiple-choice questions with custom answer options
source_title: My COMPLETE Agentic Coding Workflow to Build Anything (No Fluff or Overengineering)

claim: Use Git commit messages as long-term memory for your AI system; standardize commit messages (via a /commit command) so agents can review git log history to understand patterns and evolution
actionable: Create a /commit command that standardizes git messages; have agents read git log during planning phases to understand code evolution
source_title: My COMPLETE Agentic Coding Workflow to Build Anything (No Fluff or Overengineering)

claim: Implement system evolution mindset: when agents encounter bugs or misalignment, fix it in the AI layer (AGENTS.md rules, on-demand context, commands) not just the code to prevent repeated mistakes
actionable: After each bug or misalignment, audit your AGENTS.md, rules, and context files; add linting rules or style guides to prevent the agent from making the same mistake again
source_title: My COMPLETE Agentic Coding Workflow to Build Anything (No Fluff or Overengineering)

claim: Do not use sub-agents for implementation; sub-agents lose context of files being edited and cause hallucinations. Only use sub-agents for research and exploration
actionable: Keep implementation in main agent context; only delegate research, documentation exploration, and code base analysis to sub-agents
source_title: My COMPLETE Agentic Coding Workflow to Build Anything (No Fluff or Overengineering)

claim: Use the Prime command (/prime) at the start of every new session to explore codebase and understand current state before proceeding with implementation
actionable: Create a /prime command that reads documentation, explores code structure, checks git log, and outputs a verification report of what the agent found
source_title: My COMPLETE Agentic Coding Workflow to Build Anything (No Fluff or Overengineering)

claim: Set up environment variables (.env.example) before implementation to prevent agents from writing mock tests instead of running real database operations
actionable: Create .env.example with all required environment variables and configure them before implementation so agents can run migrations and actual tests
source_title: My COMPLETE Agentic Coding Workflow to Build Anything (No Fluff or Overengineering)
